domain_experience: 4 years listening to independent therapists and small clinic owners
deep exposure to admin, documentation, staffing, and insurance pain points
constraints: does not want client service work
unfair_advantages: large backlog of real operator pain points
ability to validate and launch without cold start distribution

opportunities.title: Practice Ops Benchmark and AI SOP Toolkit to workflow SaaS
opportunities.thesis: Start with a paid benchmark and template product for therapist practice operations, then turn the highest-retention workflow into a lightweight tracker SaaS.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user already has trusted distribution to the exact buyers, years of niche pain intelligence, and can create proprietary benchmark data that a generic AI operator cannot access.
validation_plan: Publish a landing page with a narrow promise around back-office benchmarks and SOPs
Survey newsletter and community to rank admin, insurance, staffing, and documentation pains
Pre-sell the founding version before building the full library
Deliver only the top requested assets and track repeated use
Choose the software wedge based on buyer pull
tool_specs: AI prompt packs for non-clinical internal workflows
Later SaaS tracker for denials, credentialing, staffing, or admin execution
kill_criteria: Fewer than 5 paid conversions from a warm launch
No single workflow emerges as a software wedge
Buyers mainly request custom service work
Compliance risk rises due to PHI or legal-advice drift

opportunities.title: Insurance and Admin Template Vault
opportunities.thesis: Sell a premium library of therapist-practice admin, hiring, policy, and payer-follow-up templates with AI-assisted adaptation prompts.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: Trust and domain specificity matter more than generic template volume, and this user has both the buyer channel and the editorial credibility.
validation_plan: Sell a founding bundle to the email list
Measure which templates are most requested
Use requests to inform benchmark and software roadmap
kill_criteria: High clicks but low payment intent
Users want bespoke customization rather than productized assets

opportunities.title: Staffing and Compensation Benchmark Subscription
opportunities.thesis: Build a recurring data product around therapist practice staffing, compensation, utilization, and retention.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The audience and trust make it plausible to gather private operational data that new entrants cannot easily collect.
validation_plan: Run a compensation and staffing survey
Pre-sell the report to operators before collecting all data
Test whether subscribers want annual or quarterly updates
kill_criteria: Response quality is too low to produce trusted benchmarks
Insufficient willingness to pay for recurring updates

opportunities.title: Insurance Ops Tracker for Small Practices
opportunities.thesis: Offer a narrow software tool for credentialing, claims aging, denials, appeals, and payer follow-up without trying to replace the EHR.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can pre-sell to a warm niche audience and stay focused on a specific neglected workflow instead of competing head-on with broad practice management vendors.
validation_plan: Validate the exact workflow through template and benchmark buyers
Prototype with sheets or no-code before custom build
Avoid PHI ingestion in the first version
kill_criteria: Users do not switch from spreadsheets
Integration requirements make the v1 too heavy

next_actions: Write and publish a pre-sell landing page
Survey the audience on top back-office pain
Collect payments or deposits for a founding offer
Build only the top requested assets
Select the software wedge based on repeated workflow demand
